---
title: "Can Grip-Force Dynamics Track Perceptual Strain? A Trial-Level Spectral Analysis in Older Adults"
shorttitle: "Grip-Force Spectral Dynamics and Perceptual Strain"
leadauthor: "Dastgheib"
author:
  - name: Mohammad Dastgheib
    corresponding: true
    affiliations:
      - Department of Psychology, University of California, Riverside, Riverside, CA, USA
    email: mdastgheib@ucr.edu
abstract: |
  Older adults frequently perform perceptual decisions while sustaining physical effort, yet most dual-task research treats motor output as a binary manipulation rather than a continuous behavioral signal. Here we ask whether sub-second grip-force spectral dynamics carry incremental information about trial-level perceptual accuracy. Older adults (*N* ≈ 64) performed auditory and visual same/different discrimination while maintaining low (5%) or high (40%) isometric handgrip force. Event-locked spectral power was extracted in a visuomotor tracking band (0.5–3.0 Hz) and a physiological tremor band (8.0–12.0 Hz); 37 trials identified as recording artifacts (|*z*| > 3.5 within each grip arm; max *z* ≈ 48.5) were excluded before all inferential analyses. Mixed-effects logistic regression and subject-wise cross-validation assessed whether band-power features explained accuracy beyond task difficulty. After the outlier screen, adding force features significantly improved model fit in the high-grip arm (LRT χ²(2) = 7.34, *p* = .025). Tremor-band power was negatively associated with accuracy (OR = 0.64, 95% CI [0.46, 0.89], *p* = .008) and the association was amplified at harder trials (tremor × difficulty, *p* = .005). Tracking-band power was not significant (OR = 1.03, *p* = .452). The tremor effect was not grip-specific (tremor × grip interaction, *p* = .244). Sensitivity analyses across removal thresholds and winsorization confirmed the result. Cross-validated models showed directional incremental gain (ΔAUC ≈ 0.006). These results provide a reproducible trial-level force-signal pipeline and initial evidence that tremor-band motor instability indexes perceptual strain under physical load.
keywords:
  - aging
  - dual-task
  - handgrip force
  - perceptual decision-making
  - physiological tremor
  - cognitive-motor interference
correspondence-email: mdastgheib@ucr.edu
date: "2026-06-24"
bibliography: references.bib
format:
  biorxiv-typst:
    columns: 2
    toc: false
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
---

{{< include _manuscript-content.qmd >}}
